food_subgroup: Pasta dishes with cream or white sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for penne pasta with white/béchamel sauce. FOODON_00002473 (pasta food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for penne with white sauce exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 (Pasta) used as closest parent; no specific concept for penne with white sauce.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of penne pasta with white sauce is approximately 350 g, typically served as a main course on a plate.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta with cream sauce, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; adapted for Indian-restaurant white sauce penne
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Indian-restaurant penne pasta with white sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked pasta (FDC ID 168936) and white/béchamel sauce (FDC ID 172451), combined in a representative ratio of approximately 60% cooked penne and 40% white sauce with optional chicken or vegetables. The composite reflects a typical restaurant portion composition. Energy and macros are cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy pasta with cream sauce entries. Micronutrients not reported by the composite source are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; penne pasta, cooked al dente (GI ~45)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 45 is based on penne pasta cooked al dente as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. White sauce (béchamel) is predominantly fat and protein with low carbohydrate contribution, which does not materially raise the composite GI. GL per serving calculated as: (45 × 64.75 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 29.1. Available carbohydrate per serving = 18.5 × 3.5 × (1 − 0.065) ≈ 60.5 g net; GL rounded to 29.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~1085 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single restaurant serving of penne pasta with white sauce can contribute approximately 1085 mg sodium — nearly half the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — so individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~29 per serving) from refined pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although penne pasta has a low GI (~45), the large restaurant portion delivers a moderate glycemic load of approximately 29, which can raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion or substitute with wholegrain pasta if available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and cream in white sauce (~3.8 g/100 g; ~13.3 g per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The béchamel-style white sauce contributes significant saturated fat (~13 g per serving), which exceeds half the recommended daily limit for saturated fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and request lighter sauce preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Penne Pasta with White Sauce (Vegetarian or Chicken) is a pasta main course served at Indian-influenced restaurants in Singapore, notably at Dugout on Circular Road. The dish consists of tubular penne pasta cooked al dente and coated in a rich béchamel-style white sauce made from butter, flour, milk or cream, garlic, onion, and mixed herbs. The vegetarian variant includes sautéed vegetables such as capsicum, mushroom, and broccoli, while the chicken variant adds grilled or sautéed chicken pieces. The sauce is creamy, mildly savoury, and lightly herbed, with a smooth texture that clings to the ridged pasta surface. Despite being served in an Indian restaurant context, the dish is a Western-style pasta preparation adapted for the local Singapore market. Nutritionally, a 350 g restaurant serving provides approximately 567 kcal, 21.7 g protein, 24.9 g fat (of which ~13.3 g is saturated), and 64.8 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index of penne pasta is approximately 45 (low), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~29) due to portion size. Sodium content is notable (~1085 mg per serving) and is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The saturated fat content from the cream-based sauce warrants attention for cardiovascular disease management. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, portion control is advised given the moderate glycemic load. The dish is halal-suitable (chicken variant) and contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: What is penne pasta with white sauce?
Calories in penne pasta white sauce
Is penne pasta good for diabetics?
White sauce pasta at Indian restaurant Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked penne pasta and béchamel/white sauce in a representative ratio. GI is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for penne pasta cooked al dente. Confidence is medium because the composite estimation is methodologically sound but not derived from a direct laboratory analysis of this specific dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (cooked penne pasta FDC 168936 and white sauce/béchamel FDC 172451) in an approximate 60:40 ratio. No direct HPB or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this dish. 2. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Meat and alternatives' (input) to 'Grains and staples' because pasta is the primary energy-contributing ingredient; the chicken variant is optional. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA composite source for this preparation. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA composite source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA composite source. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on penne pasta al dente from the University of Sydney GI Database; the white sauce component does not materially alter the GI. GL per serving = (45 × 64.75) / 100 ≈ 29.1 for a 350 g serving. 5. The dish is listed under Indian cuisine in field survey data due to the restaurant context (Dugout — Circular Road), but the preparation is Western-style pasta. 6. Dietary flag is_vegetarian is set to false because the dish has a chicken variant; the vegetarian variant alone would qualify as vegetarian.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that food_group needed reclassification from 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Grains and staples'. Verified all aliases_by_language entries appear in flat aliases list. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed GI category 'low' correct for GI=45 (≤55 threshold). Confirmed ontology match_type values restricted to allowed set.

Added this entry to the 'aliases' list to ensure perfect consistency. Updated iteration count and final decision to PASS.
